core.title: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization
core.abstract:
Photorealistic 3D representations are on the rise in popularity in academia and industry due to the emergence of a computationally friendly and high-fidelity method of 3D Gaussian Splatting. 3D representations are crucial for numerous tasks in virtual/augmented reality and robotics, such as virtual tours, computer-aided design, generative editing, navigation, and more. However, most researchers overlook the crucial step of the reconstruction pipeline that may affect the results and/or take a large portion of reconstruction time – initial point cloud creation with a structure-from-motion algorithm.
Our work explores the acceleration and quality of photorealistic reconstruction initialized with a point cloud obtained via depth maps unprojection using posed RGBD images captured with consumer-grade sensors found in the iPhone Pro series. We demonstrate significant acceleration of the initialization step, which is possible with additional smartphone sensors. We explore how classical point cloud postprocessing and camera pose accuracy affect the resulting 3D Gaussian Splatting reconstruction using quantitative and qualitative evaluations on an established academic indoor dataset and custom-captured data.
We also release an extendable object-oriented codebase allowing integration of custom depth, image, and camera parameter sources, with the possibility of configuring the reconstruction to simplify the end-to-end reconstruction pipeline. We aim to stimulate research in photorealistic reconstructions of non-professional scene captures made with depth-sensing scanners. Our code is available online at https://github.com/rwmutel/rgbd2colmap

core.title: HoloGS: Instant Depth-based 3D Gaussian Splatting with Microsoft HoloLens 2
core.abstract: In the fields of photogrammetry, computer vision and computer graphics, the task of neural 3D scene reconstruction has led to the exploration of various techniques. Among these, 3D Gaussian Splatting stands out for its explicit representation of scenes using 3D Gaussians, making it appealing for tasks like 3D point cloud extraction and surface reconstruction. Motivated by its potential, we address the domain of 3D scene reconstruction, aiming to leverage the capabilities of the Microsoft HoloLens 2 for instant 3D Gaussian Splatting. We present HoloGS, a novel workflow utilizing HoloLens sensor data, which bypasses the need for pre-processing steps like Structure from Motion by instantly accessing the required input data i.e. the images, camera poses and the point cloud from depth sensing. We provide comprehensive investigations, including the training process and the rendering quality, assessed through the Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio, and the geometric 3D accuracy of the densified point cloud from Gaussian centers, measured by Chamfer Distance. We evaluate our approach on two self-captured scenes: An outdoor scene of a cultural heritage statue and an indoor scene of a fine-structured plant. Our results show that the HoloLens data, including RGB images, corresponding camera poses, and depth sensing based point clouds to initialize the Gaussians, are suitable as input for 3D Gaussian Splatting

core.title:
HoloGS: Instant Depth-based 3D Gaussian Splatting with Microsoft
  HoloLens 2
core.abstract:
In the fields of photogrammetry, computer vision and computer graphics, the
task of neural 3D scene reconstruction has led to the exploration of various
techniques. Among these, 3D Gaussian Splatting stands out for its explicit
representation of scenes using 3D Gaussians, making it appealing for tasks like
3D point cloud extraction and surface reconstruction. Motivated by its
potential, we address the domain of 3D scene reconstruction, aiming to leverage
the capabilities of the Microsoft HoloLens 2 for instant 3D Gaussian Splatting.
We present HoloGS, a novel workflow utilizing HoloLens sensor data, which
bypasses the need for pre-processing steps like Structure from Motion by
instantly accessing the required input data i.e. the images, camera poses and
the point cloud from depth sensing. We provide comprehensive investigations,
including the training process and the rendering quality, assessed through the
Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio, and the geometric 3D accuracy of the densified
point cloud from Gaussian centers, measured by Chamfer Distance. We evaluate
our approach on two self-captured scenes: An outdoor scene of a cultural
heritage statue and an indoor scene of a fine-structured plant. Our results
show that the HoloLens data, including RGB images, corresponding camera poses,
and depth sensing based point clouds to initialize the Gaussians, are suitable
as input for 3D Gaussian Splatting.Comment: 8 pages, 9 figures, 2 tables. Will be published in the ISPRS The
  International Archives of Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Spatial
  Information Science

core.title: Impact of Camera Settings on 3D Reconstruction Quality: Insights from NeRF and Gaussian Splatting
core.abstract: This paper explores the influence of various camera settings on the quality of 3D reconstructions, particularly in indoor crime scene investigations. Utilizing Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) and Gaussian Splatting for 3D reconstruction, we analyzed the impact of ISO, shutter speed, and aperture settings on the quality of the resulting 3D reconstructions. By conducting controlled experiments in a meeting room setup, we identified optimal settings that minimize noise and artifacts while maximizing detail and brightness. Our findings indicate that an ISO of 200, a shutter speed of 1/60 s, and an aperture of f/3.5 provide the best balance for high-quality 3D reconstructions. These settings are especially useful for forensic applications, architectural visualization, and cultural heritage preservation, offering practical guidelines for professionals in these fields. The study also highlights the potential for future research to expand on these findings by exploring other camera parameters and real-time adjustment techniques

core.title:
GaussianRoom: Improving 3D Gaussian Splatting with SDF Guidance and
  Monocular Cues for Indoor Scene Reconstruction
core.abstract:
Recently, 3D Gaussian Splatting(3DGS) has revolutionized neural rendering
with its high-quality rendering and real-time speed. However, when it comes to
indoor scenes with a significant number of textureless areas, 3DGS yields
incomplete and noisy reconstruction results due to the poor initialization of
the point cloud and under-constrained optimization. Inspired by the continuity
of signed distance field (SDF), which naturally has advantages in modeling
surfaces, we present a unified optimizing framework integrating neural SDF with
3DGS. This framework incorporates a learnable neural SDF field to guide the
densification and pruning of Gaussians, enabling Gaussians to accurately model
scenes even with poor initialized point clouds. At the same time, the geometry
represented by Gaussians improves the efficiency of the SDF field by piloting
its point sampling. Additionally, we regularize the optimization with normal
and edge priors to eliminate geometry ambiguity in textureless areas and
improve the details. Extensive experiments in ScanNet and ScanNet++ show that
our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in both surface reconstruction
and novel view synthesis

core.title:
PGSR: Planar-based Gaussian Splatting for Efficient and High-Fidelity
  Surface Reconstruction
core.abstract:
Recently, 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has attracted widespread attention due
to its high-quality rendering, and ultra-fast training and rendering speed.
However, due to the unstructured and irregular nature of Gaussian point clouds,
it is difficult to guarantee geometric reconstruction accuracy and multi-view
consistency simply by relying on image reconstruction loss. Although many
studies on surface reconstruction based on 3DGS have emerged recently, the
quality of their meshes is generally unsatisfactory. To address this problem,
we propose a fast planar-based Gaussian splatting reconstruction representation
(PGSR) to achieve high-fidelity surface reconstruction while ensuring
high-quality rendering. Specifically, we first introduce an unbiased depth
rendering method, which directly renders the distance from the camera origin to
the Gaussian plane and the corresponding normal map based on the Gaussian
distribution of the point cloud, and divides the two to obtain the unbiased
depth. We then introduce single-view geometric, multi-view photometric, and
geometric regularization to preserve global geometric accuracy. We also propose
a camera exposure compensation model to cope with scenes with large
illumination variations. Experiments on indoor and outdoor scenes show that our
method achieves fast training and rendering while maintaining high-fidelity
rendering and geometric reconstruction, outperforming 3DGS-based and NeRF-based
methods.Comment: project page: https://zju3dv.github.io/pgsr

core.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting Rekonstruktionsmönster över scenattribut
core.abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a revolutionary method in the field of 3D scene reconstruction. By utilizing an explicit representation through a powerful graphical primitive, it has quickly become a state-of-the-art (SOTA) technique, with recent research focusing heavily on improving its resource efficiency. However, a significant drawback is the high variability in training time, memory usage, and the number of Gaussian primitives required across different scenes. This makes it difficult to predict performance and output quality for scenes that have not been previously tested. Even with the same number of Gaussian primitives, the visual quality can vary substantially between scenes. This study investigates which scene attributes influence the visual quality relative to the number of Gaussian primitives. Through experiments and detailed analysis using reconstruction accuracy heatmaps, it became evident that niche viewpoints and specific angles can cause any region of a scene to suffer from blurry artifacts and poor reconstruction. Sky regions, depending on exposure and uniformity, can be either among the easiest or most difficult parts to reconstruct, and may benefit from specialized modeling approaches. Similarly, monochrome surfaces, such as walls and matte materials, tend to be reconstructed with high accuracy. Dark or shadowed areas consistently showed strong reconstruction performance, indicating that the lack of detail is easier for 3DGS to handle. While contours are not a dominant problem, they remain a persistent challenge. Overall, indoor scenes generally outperform outdoor scenes. Although the Gaussian primitive is powerful, reflective surfaces remain difficult to accurately capture, even with view-dependent color.3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) har etablerat sig som en banbrytande metod inom 3D-återskapning av scener. Genom att använda en explicit representation med en kraftfull grafisk primitiv har tekniken snabbt blivit state-of-the-art (SOTA), och senaste forskningen har fokuserat mycket på att förbättra resurseffektiviteten. En tydlig nackdel är dock att träningstid, minnesanvändning och antalet Gaussian-primitiver varierar kraftigt mellan olika scener. Det gör det svårt att förutse prestanda och resultat för scener som inte tidigare testats. Även med samma antal Gaussian-primitiver kan den visuella kvaliteten skilja sig markant mellan olika scener. Denna studie undersöker vilka scenattribut som påverkar den visuella kvaliteten i relation till antalet Gaussian-primitiver. Genom experiment och detaljerad analys med hjälp av värmekartor över rekonstruktionsprecision blev det tydligt att nischade vyer och specifika vinklar kan leda till suddiga artefakter och låg återgivningskvalitet i vilken del av scenen som helst. Himlen kan, beroende på exponering och enhetlighet, vara antingen en av de enklaste eller svåraste delarna att återskapa, och skulle kunna hanteras bättre med specialiserade modelleringsmetoder. På liknande sätt är monokroma ytor, såsom väggar och matta material, oftast enkla att rekonstruera med hög precision. Mörka eller skuggade områden uppvisade genomgående god rekonstruktionskvalitet, vilket tyder på att detaljfattiga ytor är lättare för 3DGS att hantera. Konturer utgör inget dominerande problem men förblir en konsekvent utmaning. Generellt presterar inomhusscener bättre än utomhusscener. Trots att Gaussian-primitiven är kraftfull, är reflekterande ytor fortfarande svåra att återskapa korrekt, även med vinkelberoende färg

core.title: Evaluating Radiance Field-Inspired Methods for 3D Indoor Reconstruction: A Comparative Analysis
core.abstract: An efficient and robust solution for 3D indoor reconstruction is crucial for various managerial operations in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) sector, such as indoor asset tracking and facility management. Conventional approaches, primarily relying on SLAM and deep learning techniques, face certain limitations. With the recent emergence of radiance field (RF)-inspired methods, such as Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) and 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS), it is worthwhile to evaluate their capability and applicability for reconstructing built environments in the AEC domain. This paper aims to compare different RF-inspired methods with conventional SLAM-based methods and to assess their potential use for asset management and related downstream tasks in indoor environments. Experiments were conducted in university and laboratory settings, focusing on 3D indoor reconstruction and semantic asset segmentation. The results indicate that 3DGS and Nerfacto generally outperform other NeRF-based methods. In addition, this study provides guidance on selecting appropriate reconstruction approaches for specific use cases

core.title: Impact of Data Capture Methods on 3D Reconstruction with Gaussian Splatting
core.abstract: This study examines how different filming techniques can enhance the quality of 3D reconstructions with a particular focus on their use in indoor crime scene investigations. Using Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) and Gaussian Splatting, we explored how factors like camera orientation, filming speed, data layering, and scanning path affect the detail and clarity of 3D reconstructions. Through experiments in a mock crime scene apartment, we identified optimal filming methods that reduce noise and artifacts, delivering clearer and more accurate reconstructions. Filming in landscape mode, at a slower speed, with at least three layers and focused on key objects produced the most effective results. These insights provide valuable guidelines for professionals in forensics, architecture, and cultural heritage preservation, helping them capture realistic high-quality 3D representations. This study also highlights the potential for future research to expand on these findings by exploring other algorithms, camera parameters, and real-time adjustment techniques

core.title: Seeing beyond vegetation: A comparative occlusion analysis between Multi-View Stereo, Neural Radiance Fields and Gaussian Splatting for 3D reconstruction
core.abstract: Image-based 3D reconstruction offers realistic scene representation for applications that require accurate geometric information. Although the assumption that images are simultaneously captured, perfectly posed and noise-free simplifies the 3D reconstruction, this rarely holds in real-world settings. A real-world scene comprises multiple objects which obstruct each other and certain object parts are occluded, thus it can be challenging to generate a complete and accurate geometry. Being a part of our environment, we are particularly interested in vegetation that often obscures important structures, leading to incomplete reconstruction of the underlying features. In this contribution, we present a comparative analysis of the geometry behind vegetation occlusions reconstructed by traditional Multi-View Stereo (MVS) and radiance field methods, namely: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs), 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) and 2D Gaussian Splatting (2DGS). Excluding certain image parts and investigating how different level of vegetation occlusions affect the geometric reconstruction, we consider Synthetic masks with different occlusion coverage of 10% (Very Sparse), 30% (Sparse), 50% (Medium), 70% (Dense) and 90% (Very Dense). To additionally demonstrate the impact of spatially consistent 3D occlusions, we use Natural masks (up to 35%) where the vegetation is stationary in the 3D scene, but relative to the view-point. Our investigations are based on real-world scenarios; one occlusion-free indoor scenario, on which we apply the Synthetic masks and one outdoor scenario, from which we derive the Natural masks. The qualitative and quantitative 3D evaluation is based on point cloud comparison against a ground truth mesh addressing accuracy and completeness. The conducted experiments and results demonstrate that although MVS shows lowest accuracy errors in both scenarios, the completeness manifests a sharp decline as the occlusion percentage increases, eventually failing under Very Dense masks. NeRFs manifest robustness in the reconstruction with highest completeness considering masks, although the accuracy proportionally decreases with increasing the occlusions. 2DGS achieves second best accuracy results outperforming NeRFs and 3DGS, indicating a consistent performance across different occlusion scenarios. Additionally, by using MVS for initialization, 3DGS and 2DGS completeness improves without significantly sacrificing the accuracy, due to the more densely reconstructed homogeneous areas. We demonstrate that radiance field methods can compete against traditional MVS, showing robust performance for a complete reconstruction under vegetation occlusions

github.name: FreeSplatPP
github.description: Official Implementation of Arxiv 2025 paper 'FreeSplat++: Generalizable 3D Gaussian Splatting for Efficient Indoor Scene Reconstruction'

github.name: SfM-free-splatting
github.description: Large-scale accurate indoor gaussian splatting 3D reconstruction based on video stream

github.name: Semantic-Guided-3DGS
github.description: Semantic-Guided 3D Gaussian Splatting for Resource-Efficient Indoor Scene Reconstruction (MSc Thesis, NTU 2025)

github.name: indoor_video-3d_reconstruction
github.description: Guide and demo showing how to convert indoor walkthrough videos into 3D models using Photogrammetry (Metashape), NeRF (Nerfstudio), and 3D Gaussian Splatting (Unity viewer). Includes scripts, instructions, and demo media — no 3D model files.

github.name: gs-scenenn
github.description: This is the foundation of my ongoing dissertation on Indoor Scene Reconstruction Using 3D Gaussian Splatting

github.name: Video-to-3D-Reconstruction
github.description: This project fulfills the "Video to 3D Reconstruction" challenge by building a robust, multi-stage pipeline. It takes a raw video of an indoor scene, extracts optimal keyframes, reconstructs a highly detailed 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) model, and finally applies 3D semantic labels.

github.name: 3DGS-Scene-Reconstruction
github.description: Indoor scene reconstruction using 3D Gaussian Splatting

github.name: 3dgs-indoor-reconstruction
github.description: 3D Gaussian Splatting indoor scene reconstruction from ROS bag using OpenSplat

github.name: 3dgs-outdoor-reconstruction
github.description: End-to-end 3D Gaussian Splatting pipeline for photorealistic indoor scene reconstruction (CS5330 final project).

github.name: 3D-Mapping-and-Localization-Rover
github.description: A rover based 3D mapping system that combines SfM and 3D Gaussian Splatting for indoor scene reconstruction, localization, and edge-deployable robotic perception.

baseline_candidates.title: Evaluating Radiance Field-Inspired Methods for 3D Indoor Reconstruction: A Comparative Analysis
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly addresses 3D indoor reconstruction and likely compares methods including 3DGS, providing comparative insights.
baseline_candidates.abstract: An efficient and robust solution for 3D indoor reconstruction is crucial for various managerial operations in the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) sector, such as indoor asset tracking and facility management. Conventional approaches, primarily relying on SLAM and deep learning techniques, face certain limitations. With the recent emergence of radiance field (RF)-inspired methods, such as Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) and 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS), it is worthwhile to evaluate their capability and applicability for reconstructing built environments in the AEC domain. This pa
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: Evaluating Radiance Field-Inspired Methods for 3D Indoor Reconstruction: A Comparative Analysis is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['3d gaussian splatting', 'reconstruction']).

parallel_candidates.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting Rekonstruktionsmönster över scenattribut
parallel_candidates.reason: Uses 3DGS for scene reconstruction but focuses on reconstruction patterns over scene attributes, not specifically indoor scenes.
parallel_candidates.abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a revolutionary method in the field of 3D scene reconstruction. By utilizing an explicit representation through a powerful graphical primitive, it has quickly become a state-of-the-art (SOTA) technique, with recent research focusing heavily on improving its resource efficiency. However, a significant drawback is the high variability in training time, memory usage, and the number of Gaussian primitives required across different scenes. This makes it difficult to predict performance and output quality for scenes that have not been previously tested. Ev
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: 3D Gaussian Splatting Rekonstruktionsmönster över scenattribut addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['3d gaussian splatting', 'reconstruction'], unmatched: ['indoor scene']).

parallel_candidates.title: Impact of Data Capture Methods on 3D Reconstruction with Gaussian Splatting
parallel_candidates.reason: Uses 3DGS for reconstruction but focuses on data capture methods for indoor crime scenes, not general indoor scene reconstruction.
parallel_candidates.abstract: This study examines how different filming techniques can enhance the quality of 3D reconstructions with a particular focus on their use in indoor crime scene investigations. Using Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) and Gaussian Splatting, we explored how factors like camera orientation, filming speed, data layering, and scanning path affect the detail and clarity of 3D reconstructions. Through experiments in a mock crime scene apartment, we identified optimal filming methods that reduce noise and artifacts, delivering clearer and more accurate reconstructions. Filming in landscape mode, at a slower
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: Impact of Data Capture Methods on 3D Reconstruction with Gaussian Splatting addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['reconstruction'], unmatched: ['3d gaussian splatting', 'indoor scene']).

parallel_candidates.title: Seeing beyond vegetation: A comparative occlusion analysis between Multi-View Stereo, Neural Radiance Fields and Gaussian Splatting for 3D reconstruction
parallel_candidates.reason: Compares 3DGS for 3D reconstruction but focuses on vegetation occlusion, not specifically indoor scenes.
parallel_candidates.abstract: Image-based 3D reconstruction offers realistic scene representation for applications that require accurate geometric information. Although the assumption that images are simultaneously captured, perfectly posed and noise-free simplifies the 3D reconstruction, this rarely holds in real-world settings. A real-world scene comprises multiple objects which obstruct each other and certain object parts are occluded, thus it can be challenging to generate a complete and accurate geometry. Being a part of our environment, we are particularly interested in vegetation that often obscures important struct

parallel_candidates.title: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization
parallel_candidates.reason: Uses 3DGS but focuses on initialization acceleration, not specifically on indoor scene reconstruction.
parallel_candidates.abstract: Photorealistic 3D representations are on the rise in popularity in academia and industry due to the emergence of a computationally friendly and high-fidelity method of 3D Gaussian Splatting. 3D representations are crucial for numerous tasks in virtual/augmented reality and robotics, such as virtual tours, computer-aided design, generative editing, navigation, and more. However, most researchers overlook the crucial step of the reconstruction pipeline that may affect the results and/or take a large portion of reconstruction time – initial point cloud creation with a structure-from-motion algori
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['3d gaussian splatting', 'reconstruction'], unmatched: ['indoor scene']).

parallel_candidates.title: HoloGS: Instant Depth-based 3D Gaussian Splatting with Microsoft HoloLens 2
parallel_candidates.reason: Applies 3DGS to HoloLens 2 depth data, but not specifically focused on indoor scene reconstruction.
parallel_candidates.abstract: In the fields of photogrammetry, computer vision and computer graphics, the task of neural 3D scene reconstruction has led to the exploration of various techniques. Among these, 3D Gaussian Splatting stands out for its explicit representation of scenes using 3D Gaussians, making it appealing for tasks like 3D point cloud extraction and surface reconstruction. Motivated by its potential, we address the domain of 3D scene reconstruction, aiming to leverage the capabilities of the Microsoft HoloLens 2 for instant 3D Gaussian Splatting. We present HoloGS, a novel workflow utilizing HoloLens sensor
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: HoloGS: Instant Depth-based 3D Gaussian Splatting with Microsoft HoloLens 2 addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['3d gaussian splatting', 'reconstruction'], unmatched: ['indoor scene']).

degradation_paths: 使用公开RGB-D数据集（如ScanNet、Replica）替代自采数据
简化至单目视频输入，降低硬件依赖

innovation_points.description: 结合SDF引导和单目线索的3DGS室内重建，利用RGBD点云初始化加速训练，并引入深度先验优化几何精度
innovation_points.baseline_used: GaussianRoom: Improving 3D Gaussian Splatting with SDF Guidance and Monocular Cues for Indoor Scene Reconstruction
stitched_modules: RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization (来自Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization)
Depth-based Gaussian Initialization (来自HoloGS: Instant Depth-based 3D Gaussian Splatting with Microsoft HoloLens 2)
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 在GaussianRoom的初始化阶段，将原始SfM点云替换为RGBD-Guided初始化方法，使用RGB-D图像生成更密集的初始点云。2. 在训练过程中，引入HoloGS的深度先验损失项，与SDF引导和单目深度损失联合优化。3. 在评估时，使用室内重建标准指标（如Chamfer距离、F-score）对比基线。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization; HoloGS: Instant Depth-based 3D Gaussian Splatting with Microsoft HoloLens 2

innovation_points.description: 在3DGS室内重建中融合多视角立体（MVS）的遮挡处理策略，提升复杂场景的几何完整性
innovation_points.baseline_used: Evaluating Radiance Field-Inspired Methods for 3D Indoor Reconstruction: A Comparative Analysis
stitched_modules: Occlusion-aware sampling (来自Seeing beyond vegetation: A comparative occlusion analysis between Multi-View Stereo, Neural Radiance Fields and Gaussian Splatting for 3D reconstruction)
Data capture optimization (来自Impact of Data Capture Methods on 3D Reconstruction with Gaussian Splatting)
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 在基线3DGS训练中，集成遮挡感知采样策略，根据相机视角和深度图动态调整高斯点的采样权重。2. 采用数据捕获优化方法，在训练前对输入图像序列进行预处理（如均匀化相机轨迹、调整帧率）。3. 在室内场景数据集（如ScanNet）上评估重建的完整性和精度。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: Seeing beyond vegetation: A comparative occlusion analysis between Multi-View Stereo, Neural Radiance Fields and Gaussian Splatting for 3D reconstruction; Impact of Data Capture Methods on 3D Reconstruction with Gaussian Splatting

stitching_plan.module_b: RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization (来自Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization)
stitching_plan.module_c: Depth-based Gaussian Initialization (来自HoloGS: Instant Depth-based 3D Gaussian Splatting with Microsoft HoloLens 2)
stitching_steps: 1. 复现GaussianRoom基线：在PyTorch环境（CUDA 11.8, Python 3.8）中配置3DGS代码库，加载室内数据集（如Replica），运行原始训练脚本并记录指标。
2. 提取模块B：从Accelerating 3DGS论文中提取RGBD-Guided初始化代码，将其点云生成函数替换GaussianRoom的SfM初始化部分，确保输入为RGB-D图像。
3. 提取模块C：从HoloGS论文中提取深度先验损失函数，将其作为附加损失项加入GaussianRoom的优化循环中，与SDF损失和单目深度损失联合训练。
4. 拼接测试：在相同数据集上运行改进模型，使用Chamfer距离和PSNR评估几何和渲染质量，对比基线结果。
risk_notes: RGBD-Guided初始化需要深度图输入，若数据集无深度则需额外估计，增加预处理复杂度。
HoloGS的深度先验可能对室内场景的纹理区域过拟合，需调整损失权重。
模块拼接可能导致训练时间增加，需优化批处理大小或使用梯度累积。

comparison_papers.title: Evaluating Radiance Field-Inspired Methods for 3D Indoor Reconstruction: A Comparative Analysis
comparison_papers.reason: 直接对比多种辐射场方法在室内重建中的性能，提供基准结果和评估框架。

comparison_papers.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering
comparison_papers.reason: 3DGS原始论文，作为基础基线验证改进效果。

ablation_suggestions.name: 去除单目深度/法线约束

ablation_suggestions.name: 使用不同初始化策略（随机vs COLMAP）

ablation_suggestions.expected_drop: 随机初始化下PSNR下降3-5dB，几何指标恶化显著

experiment_checklist: 在至少3个室内数据集（如ScanNet、Replica、Matterport3D）上测试
与所有对比论文使用相同的数据划分和评估协议
报告每个指标的均值和标准差，进行多次运行
可视化重建结果（RGB、深度、法线、点云）
记录训练时间和推理速度
进行统计显著性检验（如t检验）
消融实验保持其他模块不变，仅改变目标变量

three_problems.problem: 如何结合SDF引导和单目线索，在3DGS框架下实现室内场景的精确重建？
three_problems.evidence: GaussianRoom方法已证明SDF引导和单目线索的有效性。
three_problems.from_paper: GaussianRoom: Improving 3D Gaussian Splatting with SDF Guidance and Monocular Cues for Indoor Scene Reconstruction

research_narrative.narrative_summary: 本研究针对室内场景重建中3DGS方法几何精度不足、遮挡处理困难的问题，提出融合RGBD点云初始化、深度先验、SDF引导及单目线索的改进框架。通过加速训练与几何优化，结合MVS遮挡策略，提升复杂场景的完整性。实验验证该方法在几何精度与效率上的优势，为室内重建提供新方案。

chapter_3.title: 基于RGBD点云初始化与深度先验的3DGS加速方法

chapter_4.title: 融合SDF引导与MVS遮挡处理的室内重建框架
sections: 遮挡感知采样与数据优化
整体网络架构与损失函数

research_narrative.abstract_draft: 针对室内场景重建中3DGS方法几何精度不足、遮挡处理困难的问题，本文提出DepthGS-Room框架。首先，利用RGBD点云初始化加速训练，并引入深度先验优化几何精度。其次，融合SDF引导和单目线索，提升表面重建质量。最后，结合MVS遮挡处理策略，增强复杂场景的几何完整性。实验表明，该方法在几何精度与效率上优于现有方法，为室内重建提供有效方案。

optimization_paths.direction: 融合RGBD深度先验与3DGS初始化
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 提升室内场景几何重建精度，减少浮空伪影
action_items: 采用RGBD相机（如Azure Kinect）获取深度图作为点云初始化输入
在3DGS优化过程中加入深度一致性损失
参考parallel论文中RGBD引导的点云初始化策略
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization

optimization_paths.direction: 利用单目深度估计与SDF引导优化几何
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 在无深度传感器场景下增强几何约束，提升重建完整性
action_items: 集成单目深度估计网络（如MiDaS）生成伪深度监督
引入SDF损失函数约束高斯分布的空间连续性
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: GaussianRoom: Improving 3D Gaussian Splatting with SDF Guidance and Monocular Cues for Indoor Scene Reconstruction

optimization_paths.direction: 优化数据采集策略以应对室内遮挡
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 减少因遮挡导致的空洞和几何不连续
action_items: 设计多视角、慢速、环绕式拍摄方案
对比不同相机运动轨迹对重建质量的影响
参考parallel论文中数据采集方法的比较分析
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: Impact of Data Capture Methods on 3D Reconstruction with Gaussian Splatting

degradation_paths.path: 退化为基于NeRF的室内重建方法
degradation_paths.trade_off: 牺牲渲染速度和实时性，但可能获得更稳定的几何重建

degradation_paths.path: 退化为传统MVS+深度图融合方法
degradation_paths.trade_off: 放弃神经渲染的逼真度，但依赖成熟几何管线，风险极低

risk_mitigation: 提前使用公开室内数据集（如ScanNet）验证3DGS基线性能
设置消融实验，逐步增加创新模块，确保每个改进可量化
准备多套数据采集方案（RGBD vs 纯RGB），应对传感器不可用情况
与导师定期沟通，确保创新点数量（2个）和可行性评分（65）可控

dimension_scores.reason: 创新点结合SDF引导、单目线索、RGBD初始化、MVS遮挡处理，有一定原创性，但部分思路来自已有论文，gap不够显著。
baseline仅2篇，且其中一篇为比较分析论文，方法学支撑不足；数据集缺失，硬件风险未充分规避。
三个问题分别对应工作包，但工作包描述过于简略，未明确如何解决每个问题。
叙事基本自洽，但存在过度宣传（如声称实验验证优势，实际无实验计划），且模型名称DepthGS-Room与内容关联性弱。

risks_identified: baseline数量不足（仅2篇），且包含比较分析论文，方法学支撑弱
无明确数据集，室内重建依赖RGB-D硬件，存在数据采集时间风险
工作包描述过于简略，缺乏具体实现细节
创新点部分依赖已有论文，原创性有限

research_basis.title: SDF-Guided 3DGS with Monocular Cues for Indoor Surface Reconstruction
research_basis.research_question: How does integrating SDF guidance and monocular depth/normal cues into 3D Gaussian Splatting improve indoor surface reconstruction accuracy and completeness?
research_basis.baseline: GaussianRoom: Improving 3D Gaussian Splatting with SDF Guidance and Monocular Cues for Indoor Scene Reconstruction
research_basis.experiment_metrics: GaussianRoom: Improving 3D Gaussian Splatting with SDF Guidance and Monocular Cues for Indoor Scene Reconstruction
research_basis.risk: Monocular cues may be noisy in textureless indoor regions; SDF guidance requires careful tuning of regularization weights.

research_basis.title: Planar Prior Regularization for 3DGS Indoor Surface Reconstruction
research_basis.research_question: Can explicit planar constraints (e.g., Manhattan world assumption) improve the geometric fidelity of 3D Gaussian Splatting for indoor scenes?
research_basis.baseline: PGSR: Planar-based Gaussian Splatting for Efficient and High-Fidelity Surface Reconstruction
research_basis.experiment_metrics: PGSR: Planar-based Gaussian Splatting for Efficient and High-Fidelity Surface Reconstruction
research_basis.risk: Planar priors may fail in non-Manhattan or cluttered indoor environments; requires robust plane detection.

research_basis.title: RGBD-Initialized 3DGS for Accelerated Indoor Reconstruction
research_basis.research_question: Does RGBD-guided point cloud initialization (e.g., from depth sensors) improve convergence speed and final quality of 3DGS indoor reconstruction compared to SfM initialization?
research_basis.baseline: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization
research_basis.experiment_metrics: Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting via RGBD-Guided Point Cloud Initialization
research_basis.risk: Depth sensor noise and missing depth in reflective/transparent surfaces may degrade initialization.
